records.chapter: Reward of bearing witness that there is no God except Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. al-Hassan Mu’addab from Ahmad b. Ali al-Asbahani from Ibrahim b. Muhammad al-Thaqafi from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ishaq from Abi Harun al-’Abdi from Abi Sa’eed al-Khudri who said: The Messenger of Allah (sw) was sitting on a certain day, and with him were some of his companions, among them Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS). So he (sw) said, "Whoever says 'La ilaha illallah' (there is no god but Allah) shall enter Paradise." Two men from his companions said, "We say 'La ilaha illallah'". The Messenger of Allah (sw) replied, "The testimony of 'There is no god but Allah' is accepted only from this person and his Shia (followers) who have taken the covenant of our Lord." The two men said, "We say 'La ilaha illallah'". Then the Messenger of Allah (sw) placed his hand on the head of Ali (AS) and said, "The sign (of acceptance of the statement of La ilaha illallah) is that one should not break oaths given to him, nor sit in his place or reject his words."
records.arabicText: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْمُؤَدِّبُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْأَصْبَهَانِیِّ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الثَّقَفِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ أَبِی هَارُونَ الْعَبْدِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی سَعِیدٍ الْخُدْرِیِّ قَالَ: کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص ذَاتَ یَوْمٍ جَالِساً وَ عِنْدَهُ نَفَرٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ فِیهِمْ عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَبِی طَالِبٍ ع إِذْ قَالَ مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ فَقَالَ رَجُلَانِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ فَنَحْنُ نَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّمَا تُقْبَلُ شَهَادَهُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مِنْ هَذَا وَ مِنْ شِیعَتِهِ الَّذِینَ أَخَذَ رَبُّنَا مِیثَاقَهُمْ فَقَالَ الرَّجُلَانِ فَنَحْنُ نَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهِ فَوَضَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص یَدَهُ عَلَی رَأْسِ عَلِیٍّ ع ثُمَّ قَالَ عَلَامَهُ ذَلِکَ أَنْ لَا تَحُلَّا عَقْدَهُ وَ لَا تَجْلِسَا مَجْلِسَهُ وَ لَا تُکَذِّبَا حَدِیثَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. al-Hassan Mu’addab from Ahmad b. Ali al-Asbahani from Ibrahim b. Muhammad al-Thaqafi from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ishaq from Abi Harun al-’Abdi from Abi Sa’eed al-Khudri who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) was sitting on a certain day, and with him were some of his companions, among them Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS). So he (sw) said, "Whoever says 'La ilaha illallah' (there is no god but Allah) shall enter Paradise." Two men from his companions said, "We say 'La ilaha illallah'". The Messenger of Allah (sw) replied, "The testimony of 'There is no god but Allah' is accepted only from this person and his Shia (followers) who have taken the covenant of our Lord." The two men said, "We say 'La ilaha illallah'". Then the Messenger of Allah (sw) placed his hand on the head of Ali (AS) and said, "The sign (of acceptance of the statement of La ilaha illallah) is that one should not break oaths given to him, nor sit in his place or reject his words."

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah al-malik…a hundred times
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Abi Yusif from Muhammad b. Abi ’Amira from Malik b. A’yan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If one recites a hundred times 'Laa ilaaha illallaah al-Malik Al-Haqqu Al-Mubeen' (There is no god but Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth, the Manifest), Allah, the Mighty and the Compeller, shall protect him from poverty, dispel his loneliness in his grave, grant him wealth, and open the door to Paradise (for him). ”
records.arabicText: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِی یُوسُفَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ مَالِکِ بْنِ أَعْیَنَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا الَّلهُ الْمَلِکُ الْحَقُّ الْمُبِینُ أَعَاذَهُ اللَّهُ الْعَزِیزُ الْجَبَّارُ مِنَ الْفَقْرِ وَ أَنِسَ وَحْشَهَ قَبْرِهِ وَ اسْتَجْلَبَ الْغِنَی وَ اسْتَقْرَعَ بَابَ الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Abi Yusif from Muhammad b. Abi ’Amira from Malik b. A’yan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If one recites a hundred times 'Laa ilaaha illallaah al-Malik Al-Haqqu Al-Mubeen' (There is no god but Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth, the Manifest), Allah, the Mighty and the Compeller, shall protect him from poverty, dispel his loneliness in his grave, grant him wealth, and open the door to Paradise (for him). ”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah without surprise
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Sarri from Ali b. al-Hakam from Abi al-Maghraa’ from Jabir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If one recites Laa ilaaha illallaah without surprise, the Almighty Allah will create a bird from it, which will hover over that person till the Day of Judgment and continue to remember the one who recited it (Laa ilaaha illallaah).”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ السَّرِیِّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ أَبِی الْمَغْرَاءِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مِنْ غَیْرِ تَعَجُّبٍ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْهَا طَائِراً یُرَفْرِفُ عَلَی رَأْسِ صَاحِبِهَا إِلَی أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّاعَهُ وَ یَذْکُرُ قَائِلَهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Sarri from Ali b. al-Hakam from Abi al-Maghraa’ from Jabir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If one recites Laa ilaaha illallaah without surprise, the Almighty Allah will create a bird from it, which will hover over that person till the Day of Judgment and continue to remember the one who recited it (Laa ilaaha illallaah).”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Ash-hadu al Laa ilaaha illallaah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText:
1. Muḥammad, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Najrān, from ʿAbd al-ʿAzīz al-ʿUbaydī, from ʿUmar ibn Yazīd, from Abū ʿAbdullāh al-Sadiq (as), who said: "I heard him say: Whoever says in his day, 'I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, He is One, without partner, the One God, the Eternal, who has neither a companion nor a child,' Allah will record for him forty-five million good deeds, erase from him forty-five million sins, raise him forty-five million levels, and it will be as if he has recited the entire Qur'an twelve times in one day, and a house will be built for him in Paradise."
(Ashhadu an lā ilāha illā Allāh, waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu, ilāhan wāḥidan aḥadan ṣamadan, lam yattakhidh ṣāḥibatan wa lā waladan)
records.arabicText: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِی نَجْرَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِیزِ الْعُبَیْدِیِّ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ مَنْ قَالَ فِی یَوْمِهِ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِیکَ لَهُ إِلَهاً وَاحِداً أَحَداً صَمَداً لَمْ یَتَّخِذْ صاحِبَهً وَ لا وَلَداً کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ خَمْساً وَ أَرْبَعِینَ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ حَسَنَهٍ وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ خَمْساً وَ أَرْبَعِینَ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ سَیِّئَهٍ وَ رَفَعَ لَهُ خَمْساً وَ أَرْبَعِینَ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ دَرَجَهٍ وَ کَانَ کَمَنْ قَرَأَ الْقُرْآنَ فِی یَوْمٍ اثْنَتَیْ عَشْرَهَ مَرَّهً وَ بَنَی لَهُ بَیْتاً فِی الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Muḥammad, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Najrān, from ʿAbd al-ʿAzīz al-ʿUbaydī, from ʿUmar ibn Yazīd, from Abū ʿAbdullāh al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I heard him say: Whoever says in his day, 'I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, He is One, without partner, the One God, the Eternal, who has neither a companion nor a child,' Allah will record for him forty-five million good deeds, erase from him forty-five million sins, raise him forty-five million levels, and it will be as if he has recited the entire Qur'an twelve times in one day, and a house will be built for him in Paradise."
(Ashhadu an lā ilāha illā Allāh, waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu, ilāhan wāḥidan aḥadan ṣamadan, lam yattakhidh ṣāḥibatan wa lā waladan)

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah thirty times a day
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad b. Hilal from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) who said: Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) narrates from his father, who in turn narrates from his father that a person who recites Laa ilaaha illallaahul h’aqqul mubeen thirty times a day will be given wealth, his poverty will vanish and he will knock at the door of Paradise.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی الْأَرْمَنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عِمْرَانَ الْخَرَّاطِ عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ ثَلَاثِینَ مَرَّهً لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْحَقُّ الْمُبِینُ اسْتَقْبَلَ الْغِنَی وَ اسْتَدْبَرَ الْفَقْرَ وَ قَرَعَ بَابَ الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad b. Hilal from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) who said: Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) narrates from his father, who in turn narrates from his father that a person who recites
records.thaqalaynMatn: Laa ilaaha illallaahul h’aqqul mubeen thirty times a day will be given wealth, his poverty will vanish and he will knock at the door of Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbeehaat-e-Arba often
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said, “Recite Sub-h’aanallaahi wal h’amdu lillaahi wa  laa  ilaaha  illallaahu  wallaahu  akbar  often because this statement will come to you surrounded by angels and will be included among your good deeds.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبِی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَکْثِرُوا مِنْ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ فَإِنَّهُنَّ یَأْتِینَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ لَهُنَّ مُقَدِّمَاتٌ وَ مُؤَخِّرَاتٌ وَ مُعَقِّبَاتٌ وَ هُنَ الْباقِیاتُ الصَّالِحاتُ*

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaahu h’aqqan h’aqqan
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from Bishr al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) who said: “Whoever says fifteen times every day, 'Laa ilaaha illallaahu haqqan haqqan, Laa ilaaha illallaahu eemaanan wa tasdeeqan, Laa ilaaha illallaahu 'uboodiyyatan wa riqan' (There is no god but Allah, truly, truly. There is no god but Allah, in belief and affirmation. There is no god but Allah, in servitude and submission), the Almighty Allah will turn towards him and won’t turn back from his face till he enters Paradise.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی الْأَرْمَنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عِمْرَانَ الْخَرَّاطِ عَنْ بِشْرٍ الْأَوْزَاعِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ خَمْسَ عَشْرَهَ مَرَّهً لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ حَقّاً حَقّاً لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ إِیمَاناً وَ تَصْدِیقاً لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ عُبُودِیَّهً وَ رِقّاً أَقْبَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ بِوَجْهِهِ فَلَمْ یَصْرِفْ عَنْهُ وَجْهَهُ حَتَّی یَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from Bishr al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whoever says fifteen times every day, 'Laa ilaaha illallaahu haqqan haqqan, Laa ilaaha illallaahu eemaanan wa tasdeeqan, Laa ilaaha illallaahu 'uboodiyyatan wa riqan' (There is no god but Allah, truly, truly. There is no god but Allah, in belief and affirmation. There is no god but Allah, in servitude and submission), the Almighty Allah will turn towards him and won’t turn back from his face till he enters Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of ending a supplication with Maashaa allaah…
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ibrahim b. Muhammad from ’Imran al-Za’farani from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:   “One who supplicates and says: Maashaa allaahu laa h’aula wa laa quwwata illaa billaah at the end of his supplication, his wish would be fulfilled.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عِمْرَانَ الزَّعْفَرَانِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ دَعَا فَخَتَمَ بِقَوْلِ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ إِلَّا أُجِیبَتْ حَاجَتُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Salamah b. al-Khattab from Ibrahim b. Muhammad from ’Imran al-Za’farani from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who supplicates and says: Maashaa allaahu laa h’aula wa laa quwwata illaa billaah at the end of his supplication, his wish would be fulfilled.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Alh’amdulillaahi a’laa…seven times a day
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Muhammad b. Uthman from Burayd from his brother al-Hussain from Umar b. Bazee’ from the one who mentioned it from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites: Al- h’amdulillaahi a’laa kulli ni’matin kaanat wa hiyaa kaa-inah seven times a day, has thanked (Allah) for the past and he has thanked (Allah) for the future.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ بُرَیْدٍ عَنْ أَخِیهِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ بَزِیعٍ عَمَّنْ ذَکَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَی کُلِّ نِعْمَهٍ کَانَتْ أَوْ هِیَ کَائِنَهٌ فَقَدْ أَدَّی شُکْرَ مَا مَضَی وَ شُکْرَ مَا بَقِیَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Muhammad b. Uthman from Burayd from his brother al-Hussain from Umar b. Bazee’ from the one who mentioned it from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites: Al- h’amdulillaahi a’laa kulli ni’matin kaanat wa hiyaa kaa-inah seven times a day, has thanked (Allah) for the past and he has thanked (Allah) for the future.”

records.chapter: Reward of bearing witness to the Oneness of Almighty God and prophethood of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Hilal from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from Bishr al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) who said: “One who bears witness saying, Laa ilaaha illallaah and does not say Muhammadur rasoolullaah gets ten rewards. However, if he says Laa ilaaha illallaah as well as Muhammadur rasoolullaah he would get a million rewards.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی الْأَرْمَنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عِمْرَانَ الْخَرَّاطِ عَنْ بِشْرٍ الْأَوْزَاعِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ شَهِدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ لَمْ یَشْهَدْ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص کُتِبَ لَهُ عَشْرُ حَسَنَاتٍ فَإِنْ شَهِدَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اللَّهِ کُتِبَ لَهُ ألفی [أَلْفَا] أَلْفِ حَسَنَهٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Hilal from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from Bishr al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) who said: “One who bears witness saying, Laa ilaaha illallaah and does not say Muhammadur rasoolullaah gets ten rewards. However, if he says Laa ilaaha illallaah as well as
records.thaqalaynMatn: Muhammadur rasoolullaah he would get a million rewards.”

records.chapter: Reward of bearing witness to the Oneness of Almighty God and prophethood of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad and Ibrahim b. Hashim and al-Hassan b. Ali al-Kufi from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his father from Abi Hazim al-Muzani from Sahl b. Sa’d al-Ansari who said: “I asked the meaning of this verse Wa maa kunta bijaanibit’ t’oori idh naadainaa from the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.).” The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) replied, “Two thousand years before the creation of man, the Almighty Allah wrote this verse on a dry leaf and hung it in the heaven and then gave a call, ‘O Ummah of Muhammad! My mercy precedes My anger. I grant you before you ask. I forgive you before you repent. If one of you meets Me and bears witness that there is no Lord except Me and that Muhammad is My servant and messenger I will make him enter Paradise through My mercy’.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیِّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی حَازِمٍ الْمُزَنِیِّ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ الْأَنْصَارِیِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ- وَ ما کُنْتَ بِجانِبِ الطُّورِ إِذْ نادَیْنا قَالَ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ کِتَاباً قَبْلَ أَنْ یَخْلُقَ الْخَلْقَ بِأَلْفَیْ عَامٍ فِی وَرَقِ آسٍ أَنْبَتَهُ ثُمَّ وَضَعَهَا عَلَی الْعَرْشِ ثُمَّ نَادَی یَا أُمَّهَ مُحَمَّدٍ إِنَّ رَحْمَتِی سَبَقَتْ غَضَبِی أَعْطَیْتُکُمْ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَسْأَلُونِی وَ غَفَرْتُ لَکُمْ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَسْتَغْفِرُونِی فَمَنْ لَقِیَنِی مِنْکُمْ یَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنَا وَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً عَبْدِی وَ رَسُولِی أَدْخَلْتُهُ الْجَنَّهَ بِرَحْمَتِی.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad and Ibrahim b. Hashim and al-Hassan b. Ali al-Kufi from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his father from Abi Hazim al-Muzani from Sahl b. Sa’d al-Ansari who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “I asked the meaning of this verse Wa maa kunta bijaanibit’ t’oori idh naadainaa from the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.).” The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) replied, “Two thousand years before the creation of man, the Almighty Allah wrote this verse on a dry leaf and hung it in the heaven and then gave a call, ‘O Ummah of Muhammad! My mercy precedes My anger. I grant you before you ask. I forgive you before you repent. If one of you meets Me and bears witness that there is no Lord except Me and that Muhammad is My servant and messenger I will make him enter Paradise through My mercy’.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Takbeer, Tasbeeh, Tahmeed and Tahleel a hundred times
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Muhammad ibn Musa ibn al-Mutawakkil (ra) narrated to me, saying: Ali ibn al-Husayn al-Sa'dabadi narrated to me from Ahmad ibn Abi Abdullah al-Barqi, who said: My father narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr from Malik ibn Anas from Abu Abdullah (as), from his forefathers regarding the Prophet (saw): "The poor came to the Messenger of Allah and said, 'O Messenger of Allah, the wealthy have what they can free as slaves, and we do not. They have what they can perform Hajj with, and we do not. They have what they can give in charity, and we do not. They have what they can struggle with in jihad, and we do not.' The Prophet (saw) said, 'Whoever glorifies Allah a hundred times, it is better than freeing a hundred slaves; whoever praises Allah a hundred times, it is better than leading a hundred camels; whoever says "La ilaha illa Allah" a hundred times, is the best in deeds on that day, except for someone who does more.' The rich heard about this and did the same. The poor returned to the Prophet and said, 'O Messenger of Allah, the wealthy have heard what you said and are doing it too.' He replied, 'That is the favor of Allah, which He gives to whom He wills, and Allah is the possessor of great favor.'"
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبِی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مَالِکِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع عَنِ النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ: جَاءَ الْفُقَرَاءُ إِلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص فَقَالُوا یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ لِلْأَغْنِیَاءِ مَا یُعْتِقُونَ وَ لَیْسَ لَنَا وَ لَهُمْ مَا یَحُجُّونَ وَ لَیْسَ لَنَا وَ لَهُمْ مَا یَتَصَدَّقُونَ وَ لَیْسَ لَنَا وَ لَهُمْ مَا یُجَاهِدُونَ بِهِ وَ لَیْسَ لَنَا فَقَالَ النَّبِیُّ ص مَنْ کَبَّرَ اللَّهَ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ عِتْقِ مِائَهِ رَقَبَهٍ وَ مَنْ سَبَّحَ اللَّهَ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ سِیَاقِ مِائَهِ بَدَنَهٍ وَ مَنْ حَمَّدَ اللَّهَ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ حُمْلَانِ مِائَهِ فَرَسٍ فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ بِسُرُجِهَا وَ لُجُمِهَا وَ رُکُبِهَا وَ مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ أَفْضَلَ النَّاسِ عَمَلًا فِی ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمِ إِلَّا مَنْ زَادَ قَالَ فَبَلَغَ ذَلِکَ الْأَغْنِیَاءَ فَصَنَعُوهُ قَالَ فَعَادُوا إِلَی النَّبِیِّ ص فَقَالُوا یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَدْ بَلَغَ الْأَغْنِیَاءَ مَا قُلْتَ فَصَنَعُوهُ قَالَ ذلِکَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ یُؤْتِیهِ مَنْ یَشاءُ وَ اللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِیمِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Muhammad ibn Musa ibn al-Mutawakkil (ra) narrated to me, saying: Ali ibn al-Husayn al-Sa'dabadi narrated to me from Ahmad ibn Abi Abdullah al-Barqi, who said: My father narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr from Malik ibn Anas from Abu Abdullah (as), from his forefathers regarding the Prophet (saw):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The poor came to the Messenger of Allah and said, 'O Messenger of Allah, the wealthy have what they can free as slaves, and we do not. They have what they can perform Hajj with, and we do not. They have what they can give in charity, and we do not. They have what they can struggle with in jihad, and we do not.' The Prophet (saw) said, 'Whoever glorifies Allah a hundred times, it is better than freeing a hundred slaves; whoever praises Allah a hundred times, it is better than leading a hundred camels; whoever says "La ilaha illa Allah" a hundred times, is the best in deeds on that day, except for someone who does more.' The rich heard about this and did the same. The poor returned to the Prophet and said, 'O Messenger of Allah, the wealthy have heard what you said and are doing it too.' He replied, 'That is the favor of Allah, which He gives to whom He wills, and Allah is the possessor of great favor.'"

records.chapter: Reward of Tasbeehaat-e-Arbaa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Muhammad ibn al-Husayn narrated to me, saying: Sa’d ibn Abdullah narrated to me from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Faddal, from Abi Dawud al-Mustariq, from Tha’laba ibn Maymun, from Yunus ibn Ya’qub, from Abu Abdillah al-Sadiq (as), who said: "The Messenger of Allah (saw) turned to his companions and said: 'Take up shields.' They asked, 'O Messenger of Allah, is it from an enemy who has surrounded us?' He replied, 'No, but from the fire (of Hell). Say: Subhanallah (Glory be to Allah), Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), La ilaha illa Allah (There is no god but Allah), and Allahu Akbar (Allah is the Greatest).'"
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ أَبِی دَاوُدَ الْمُسْتَرِقِّ عَنْ ثَعْلَبَهَ بْنِ مَیْمُونٍ عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ یَعْقُوبَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: الْتَفَتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِلَی أَصْحَابِهِ فَقَالَ اتَّخِذُوا جُنَناً فَقَالُوا یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَ مِنْ عَدُوٍّ قَدْ أَظَلَّنَا فَقَالَ لَا وَ لَکِنْ مِنَ النَّارِ قُولُوا سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Muhammad ibn al-Husayn narrated to me, saying: Sa’d ibn Abdullah narrated to me from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Faddal, from Abi Dawud al-Mustariq, from Tha’laba ibn Maymun, from Yunus ibn Ya’qub, from Abu Abdillah al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (saw) turned to his companions and said: 'Take up shields.' They asked, 'O Messenger of Allah, is it from an enemy who has surrounded us?' He replied, 'No, but from the fire (of Hell). Say: Subhanallah (Glory be to Allah), Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), La ilaha illa Allah (There is no god but Allah), and Allahu Akbar (Allah is the Greatest).'"

records.chapter: Reward of Tasbeehaat-e-Arbaa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said, “Recite Sub-h’aanallaahi wal h’amdu lillaahi wa  laa  ilaaha  illallaahu  wallaahu  akbar  often because this statement will come to you surrounded by angels and will be included among your good deeds.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ بَزِیعٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ یُونُسَ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَکْثِرُوا مِنْ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ فَإِنَّهُنَّ یَأْتِینَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ لَهُنَّ مُقَدِّمَاتٌ وَ مُؤَخِّرَاتٌ وَ مُعَقِّبَاتٌ وَ هُنَ الْباقِیاتُ الصَّالِحاتُ

records.chapter: Reward of Tasbeehaat-e-Arbaa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3.  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) says, “One who says Sub- h’aanallah Allah will plant a tree for him in Paradise. One who says Al-h’amdulillaah  then too a tree will be planted for  him  in  Paradise.  Similarly,  One  who  says  Laa  ilaaha illallaah then also Allah will plant a tree for him in Paradise. Allah  will  also  plant  a  tree  for  one  who  recites  Allaahu Akbar.  A man of Quraish said: O the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.), then there would be many trees in Paradise for us? Yes,  but  be  careful  that  you  do  not  send  fire  or  they  all would get burnt up. That is why the Almighty Allah said: O you who believe! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not make your deeds of no effect.1
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبِی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْجَارُودِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ غَرَسَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِهَا شَجَرَهً فِی الْجَنَّهِ وَ مَنْ قَالَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ غَرَسَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِهَا شَجَرَهً فِی الْجَنَّهِ وَ مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ غَرَسَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِهَا شَجَرَهً فِی الْجَنَّهِ وَ مَنْ قَالَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ غَرَسَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِهَا شَجَرَهً فِی الْجَنَّهِ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ قُرَیْشٍ یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ شَجَرَنَا فِی الْجَنَّهِ لَکَثِیرٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ لَکِنْ إِیَّاکُمْ أَنْ تُرْسِلُوا عَلَیْهَا نِیرَاناً فَتُحْرِقُوهَا وَ ذَلِکَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ یَقُولُ- یا أَیُّهَا الَّذِینَ آمَنُوا أَطِیعُوا اللَّهَ وَ أَطِیعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَ لا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَکُمْ.

records.chapter: Reward of Tasbeehaat-e-Arbaa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4. And with this chain of narration from Ahmad, from his father, and Muhammad ibn 'Isa, from Safwan ibn Yahya, from Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (AS), who said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (sw) said to his companions one day, 'Do you think that if you were to gather what you have of clothing and utensils, then placed them atop one another, would you perceive them reaching the sky?' They replied, 'No, O Messenger of Allah.' He said, 'Shall I not inform you of something whose root is in the earth and its branches in the sky?' They said, 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' He said, 'One of you, upon completing the obligatory prayer, should say: Subhan Allah (Glory be to Allah), Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), La ilaha illallah (There is no god but Allah), Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greater than what can be described); thirty times. For indeed, their root is in the earth and their branches are in the sky. They prevent destruction, fire, drowning, falling in a well, consumption by wild beasts, death due to accidents, and the affliction that descends from the sky upon the servant on that day. They are the enduring good deeds.'"
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ الْخَزَّازِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص قَالَ لِأَصْحَابِهِ ذَاتَ یَوْمٍ أَ رَأَیْتُمْ لَوْ جَمَعْتُمْ مَا عِنْدَکُمْ مِنَ الثِّیَابِ وَ الْآنِیَهِ ثُمَّ وَضَعْتُمْ بَعْضَهُ عَلَی بَعْضٍ أَ کُنْتُمْ تَرَوْنَهُ تَبْلُغُ السَّمَاءَ قَالُوا لَا یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ أَ لَا أَدُلُّکُمْ عَلَی شَیْ ءٍ أَصْلُهُ فِی الْأَرْضِ وَ فَرْعُهُ فِی السَّمَاءِ قَالُوا بَلَی یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ یَقُولُ أَحَدُکُمْ إِذَا فَرَغَ مِنْ صَلَاهِ الْفَرِیضَهِ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ ثَلَاثِینَ مَرَّهً فَإِنَّ أَصْلَهُنَّ فِی الْأَرْضِ وَ فَرْعَهُنَّ فِی السَّمَاءِ وَ هُنَّ یَدْفَعْنَ الْهَدْمَ وَ الْحَرَقَ وَ الْغَرَقَ وَ التَّرَدِّیَ فِی الْبِئْرِ وَ أَکْلَ السَّبُعِ وَ مِیتَهَ السَّوْءِ وَ الْبَلِیَّهَ الَّتِی تَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ عَلَی الْعَبْدِ فِی ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمِ وَ هُنَ الْباقِیاتُ الصَّالِحاتُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. And with this chain of narration from Ahmad, from his father, and Muhammad ibn 'Isa, from Safwan ibn Yahya, from Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (AS), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (sw) said to his companions one day, 'Do you think that if you were to gather what you have of clothing and utensils, then placed them atop one another, would you perceive them reaching the sky?' They replied, 'No, O Messenger of Allah.' He said, 'Shall I not inform you of something whose root is in the earth and its branches in the sky?' They said, 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' He said, 'One of you, upon completing the obligatory prayer, should say: Subhan Allah (Glory be to Allah), Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), La ilaha illallah (There is no god but Allah), Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greater than what can be described); thirty times. For indeed, their root is in the earth and their branches are in the sky. They prevent destruction, fire, drowning, falling in a well, consumption by wild beasts, death due to accidents, and the affliction that descends from the sky upon the servant on that day. They are the enduring good deeds.'"

records.chapter: Reward of saying Sub-h’aanallah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad who said it was narrated to us by Abi from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites: Sub- h’aanallahi wa bih’amdihi sub-h’aanallaahil a’z’eemi wa bih’amdih, the Almighty Allah will grant him three thousand rewards, increase three thousand grades and create a bird for him, which will glorify God and its reward will be given to that person.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبِی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَ بِحَمْدِهِ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِیمِ وَ بِحَمْدِهِ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ثَلَاثَهَ آلَافِ حَسَنَهٍ وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ ثَلَاثَهَ آلَافِ سَیِّئَهٍ وَ رَفَعَ لَهُ ثَلَاثَهَ آلَافِ دَرَجَهٍ وَ یَخْلُقُ مِنْهَا طَائِراً فِی الْجَنَّهِ یُسَبِّحُ وَ کَانَ أَجْرُ تَسْبِیحِهِ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad who said it was narrated to us by Abi from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites: Sub- h’aanallahi wa bih’amdihi sub-h’aanallaahil a’z’eemi wa bih’amdih, the Almighty Allah will grant him three thousand rewards, increase three thousand grades and create a bird for him, which will glorify God and its reward will be given to that person.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbeeh without surprise
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from his father and al-Hassan b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Sinan from Abi al-Jarud from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “For one who recites Sub-h’aanallaah without surprise Allah will create a bird from it, having a tongue and two wings. It will glorify Allah for that person till the Day of Judgment. The recitation of statement Al-h’amdulillaahi wa laa ilaaha illallaahu wallaahu akbar also holds the same reward.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْجَارُودِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ مِنْ غَیْرِ تَعَجُّبٍ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْهَا طَائِراً لَهُ لِسَانَانِ وَ حَاجِبَانِ یُسَبِّحُ اللَّهَ عَنْهُ فِی الْمُسَبِّحِینَ حَتَّی تَقُومَ السَّاعَهُ وَ مِثْلُ ذَلِکَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ اللَّهُ أَکْبَرُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from his father and al-Hassan b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Sinan from Abi al-Jarud from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “For one who recites Sub-h’aanallaah without surprise Allah will create a bird from it, having a tongue and two wings. It will glorify Allah for that person till the Day of Judgment. The recitation of statement Al-h’amdulillaahi wa laa ilaaha illallaahu wallaahu akbar also holds the same reward.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Sub-h’aanallaah a hundred times
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Ibn Fadhal from Yunis b. Ya’qub who said: Yunus bin Yaqoob says that he asked Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.), “[O son of Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.)] One who recites Sub-h’aanallaah a hundred times, will he be counted among the ones who remember Allah a lot?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “Yes.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ یَعْقُوبَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ مِمَّنْ ذَکَرَ اللَّهَ کَثِیراً قَالَ نَعَمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Ibn Fadhal from Yunis b. Ya’qub who said: Yunus bin Yaqoob says that he asked Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.), “[O son of Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.)]
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who recites Sub-h’aanallaah a hundred times, will he be counted among the ones who remember Allah a lot?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “Yes.”

records.chapter: Reward of saying Al-h’amdulillaahi kamaa huwa ahluh
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakkil narrated to me that Ali b. al-Husayn al-Sa'dabadi narrated to him, from Ahmad b. Abi Abdullah, from Ali b. al-Hakam, from Saif b. Amirah, from Zaid al-Shahham that Abu 'Abdillah (al-Sadiq) (as) said: "Whoever says 'Praise be to Allah as He deserves', Allah will keep the scribes of heaven busy." It was asked, "How do the scribes of the heavens remain busy?". He said, "They say, 'O Allah! We do not know the unseen.' So Allah says, 'Write it as my servant said it, and for him is its reward.'"
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ زَیْدٍ الشَّحَّامِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عليه السلام قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ کَمَا هُوَ أَهْلُهُ شَغَلَ اللَّهُ کُتَّابَ السَّمَاءِ قِیلَ وَ کَیْفَ یَشْغَلُ کُتَّابَ السَّمَاءِ قَالَ یَقُولُونَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا لَا نَعْلَمُ الْغَیْبَ فَقَالَ فَیَقُولُ اکْتُبُوهَا کَمَا قَالَهَا عَبْدِی وَ عَلَیَّ ثَوَابُهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakkil narrated to me that Ali b. al-Husayn al-Sa'dabadi narrated to him, from Ahmad b. Abi Abdullah, from Ali b. al-Hakam, from Saif b. Amirah, from Zaid al-Shahham that Abu 'Abdillah (al-Sadiq) (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever says 'Praise be to Allah as He deserves', Allah will keep the scribes of heaven busy." It was asked, "How do the scribes of the heavens remain busy?". He said, "They say, 'O Allah! We do not know the unseen.' So Allah says, 'Write it as my servant said it, and for him is its reward.'"

records.chapter: Reward of saying Alhamdulillaahi rabbila’alameen four times
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Mansur b. al-Abbas from Sa’eed b. Janah who said it was narrated to me by Abu Mash’ar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Alhamdulillaahi rabbil a’alameen four times every morning has without any doubt thanked for this day and one who says it in the night has thanked for that night.
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ الْعَبَّاسِ عَنْ سَعِیدِ بْنِ جَنَاحٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو مَشْعَرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ إِذَا أَصْبَحَ أَرْبَعَ مَرَّاتٍ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِینَ فَقَدْ أَدَّی شُکْرَ یَوْمِهِ وَ مَنْ قَالَهَا إِذَا أَمْسَی فَقَدْ أَدَّی شُکْرَ لَیْلَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Mansur b. al-Abbas from Sa’eed b. Janah who said it was narrated to me by Abu Mash’ar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Alhamdulillaahi rabbil a’alameen four times every morning has without any doubt thanked for this day and one who says it in the night has thanked for that night.

records.chapter: Reward of glorifying Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from his father from Fadhalah b. Ayoob’ from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Muhammad b. Marwan from Zurarah who said: Zurarah narrates that he asked Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.), “What deed Allah likes best?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “His glorification.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ فَضَالَهَ بْنِ أَیُّوبَ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنْ زُرَارَهَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع أَیُّ الْأَعْمَالِ أَحَبُّ إِلَی اللَّهِ قَالَ أَنْ تُمَجِّدَ اللَّهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Ahmad b. Muhammad from his father from Fadhalah b. Ayoob’ from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Muhammad b. Marwan from Zurarah who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Zurarah narrates that he asked Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.), “What deed Allah likes best?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “His glorification.”

records.chapter: Reward of glorifying Divinity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says  that  the  Almighty  Allah glorifies  Himself  thrice  every  day  and  night.  One  who glorifies Him like this will have good fortune if he had a bad one.  Zurarah  asked,  “How  to  glorify  Him?”  Imam  (a.s.) replied, “Say: antallaahu laa ilaaha illar rabbul a’alameena antallaahu   laa   ilaaha   illaa   antar   rah’maanar   rah’eema antallaahu  laa  ilaaha  illaa  anta  a’liyyil  kabeeri  antallaahu laa  ilaaha  illaa  anta  maaliki  yaumiddeeni  antallaahu  laa ilaaha  illaa  antal  ghafoorur  rah’eemi  antallaahu  laa  ilaaha illaa antal a’zeezul h’akeemi antallaahu laa ilaaha illaa anta minka  bad-u  kulli  shai-inw  wa  ilaika  ya-oo’da  antallaahu laa ilaaha illaa anta lam tazal wa laa tazaalu antallaahu laa ilaaha illaa anta khaaliqul khairi wash sharru antallaahu laa ilaaha  illaa  anta  khaaliqul  jannati  wannaari  antallaahu  laa ilaaha illaa antal ah’adus’ s’amadu lam yalid wa lam yoolad wa  lam  yakullahu  kufuwan  ah’adu  antallaahu  laa  ilaaha illaa    antal    malikul    quddoosus    salaamul    mu-minul muhaiminul     a’zeezul     jabbaarul     mutakabbiru     sub- h’aanallaahi   a’mmaa   yushrikoona   antallaahul   khaaliqul baari-ul mus’awwiru lakal asmaa-ul h’usnaa yusabbih’u laka maa  fis  samaawaati  wal  arz”i  wa  antal  a’zeezul  h’akeemi antallaahu  laa  ilaaha  illaa  antal  kabeeru  wal  kibri-yaa-u ridaa-uk.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَالَهَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بُکَیْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَهَ بْنِ أَعْیَنَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ یُمَجِّدُ نَفْسَهُ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ وَ لَیْلَهٍ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ فَمَنْ مَجَّدَ اللَّهَ بِمَا مَجَّدَ بِهِ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ کَانَ فِی حَالِ شِقْوَهٍ حُوِّلَ إِلَی سَعَادَهٍ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ کَیْفَ هُوَ التَّمْجِیدُ قَالَ تَقُولُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِینَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الرَّحْمَنُ الرَّحِیمُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْعَلِیُّ الْکَبِیرُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مَلِکُ یَوْمِ الدِّینِ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِیمُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْعَزِیزُ الْحَکِیمُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ بَدْءُ کُلِّ شَیْ ءٍ وَ إِلَیْکَ یَعُودُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ لَمْ تَزَلْ وَ لَا تَزَالُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خَالِقُ الْخَیْرِ وَ الشَّرِّ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خَالِقُ الْجَنَّهِ وَ النَّارِ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْأَحَدُ الصَّمَدُ لَمْ یَلِدْ وَ لَمْ یُولَدْ. وَ لَمْ یَکُنْ لَهُ کُفُواً أَحَدٌ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْمَلِکُ الْقُدُّوسُ السَّلامُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْمُهَیْمِنُ الْعَزِیزُ الْجَبَّارُ الْمُتَکَبِّرُ سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا یُشْرِکُونَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ الْخالِقُ الْبارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ لَکَ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَی یُسَبِّحُ لَکَ مَا فِی السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ وَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِیزُ الْحَکِیمُ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ الْکَبِیرُ الْکِبْرِیَاءُ رِدَاؤُکَ.

records.chapter: Reward of a wise person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “God-willing, a wise person will end up in Paradise.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ أَبِی مُحَمَّدٍ الرَّازِیِّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ بَکْرِ بْنِ سَمَّالٍ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ مَنْ کَانَ عَاقِلًا خُتِمَ لَهُ بِالْجَنَّهِ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of a wise person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “A wise person is religious and a religious person will enter Paradise.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ کَانَ عَاقِلًا کَانَ لَهُ دِینٌ وَ مَنْ کَانَ لَهُ دِینٌ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ.
